title: ammannia coccinea subsp. pubiflora
intro: Ammannia coccinea subsp. pubiflora is a plant species within the family Lythraceae, commonly known as the West Indian Sea Island or Scarlet Ammannia. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which provide several health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from pain and discomfort. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to leverage its medicinal properties.
